title: refund — subprocessors
description: The Refund module itself engages ZERO third-party subprocessors — every state transition happens within the Academorix platform. Downstream payment module engages the actual payment providers (Stripe / Paddle / Adyen / Square / PayPal / CustomWebhook) that receive the refund dispatch; downstream marketing module engages ad-network subprocessors (Meta / Google / TikTok / Microsoft / Snapchat) that receive negative-value conversion events; downstream notifications module engages email/SMS subprocessors. All are declared in their respective modules' subprocessors.json.

downstream_subprocessors_referenced.used_for: Forwarding refund dispatch to the payment provider (Stripe / Paddle / Adyen / Square / PayPal / CustomWebhook). RefundProviderExecutor delegates to payment::PaymentProviderManager which handles the provider-side HTTP call + webhook signature verification.

Forwarding Refund Issued (negative-value conversion) events to ad networks (Meta CAPI, Google Ads Enhanced Conversions, TikTok Events API, Microsoft UET, Snapchat CAPI) for ROAS adjustment + fraud model retraining.

Delivering RefundRequestedNotification + RefundApprovalRequiredNotification + RefundApprovedNotification + RefundProcessedNotification + RefundSucceededNotification + RefundFailedNotification + RefundRejectedNotification via email / SMS / in-app / push.
CreditNoteIssuer creates the reversal credit note document. Invoice module engages document rendering + storage subprocessors (S3 for PDF hosting).
Retention pipeline (PurgeExpiredRefundDataJob) migrates records to compliance_archive S3 buckets before hard-delete. Compliance module engages the archive-storage subprocessor.

notes: This file exists deliberately with an empty subprocessors array. The GDPR Art. 30 record of processing activities requires per-module subprocessor lists; an empty list is the correct declaration for a module that engages none directly.

The Wave 4 tenant-facing 'subprocessor disclosure' page reads THIS FILE'S downstream_subprocessors_referenced list to compose the full disclosure — every downstream that this module transitively engages is surfaced to the tenant admin.

Payment provider subprocessors (Stripe / Paddle / Adyen / Square / PayPal) are the HIGH-RISK category — they receive PCI-adjacent data (payment token, refund amount, reason). Refund does not directly engage them; the payment module handles the provider-side HTTP contracts + credential management.

Ad-network subprocessors (Meta / Google / TikTok / Microsoft / Snapchat) receive PII (hashed) + click IDs + refund amounts on RefundSucceeded. Tenants opt into which providers via marketing module's provider configuration.
